title: Coelogyne subcalceata
taxonomy: Coelogyne subcalceata belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the genus Coelogyne. It is native to the Himalayas and parts of China. As for its classification within the Coelogyne genus, it is often considered an orchid species due to its unique characteristics and physical attributes. However, further classification within the Coelogyne genus might require more specific information. It's worth noting that classifications can evolve as new research emerges, so further study may alter our understanding of this plant.
uses: Coelogyne subcalceata, a species of orchid, has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of Asia. intro: Coelogyne subcalceata, commonly known as the pendant orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne subcalceata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
